scoring_model_policy.deterministic_gate2: All grid arithmetic (counts, row sums, MCR consistency) is verified in Python at Gate 2, not scored by an LLM.
scoring_model_policy.claude-sonnet-4-6: The final decision-with-reasoning item (B25) and any tie-breaking justification.

aicft_policy: This rubric produces a worksheet-level credit band only. It is NOT an AI-CFT level. AI-CFT level is cumulative and assigned at the portfolio level under researcher judgement. Per item, Gate 4 emits LO evidence (confirmed / weak / none); it never assigns a level from a single worksheet.

context.description: Student selects a feature of their own choice and tries multiple threshold values. For each threshold trial: record (1) variable name, (2) left-branch threshold (≤), (3) right-branch threshold (>), (4) recommended count on left, (5) not-recommended count on left, (6) recommended count on right, (7) not-recommended count on right. Calculate misclassification count. Then write final chosen threshold in B25.
context.grid_structure: 5 rows x 5 columns = 25 blanks. Each row = one threshold trial. Columns per row: (1) variable name, (2) left threshold, (3) right threshold, (4) correctly classified left, (5) correctly classified right — OR (1) variable, (2) ≤ threshold, (3) > threshold, (4-5) counts left, (6-7) counts right, (8) MCR.
context.note: Exact column structure depends on worksheet version. Item IDs B1-B24 = grid cells, B25 = final choice with reasoning.

credit_rules.full: Names one of the 7 valid features from the food card dataset.
credit_rules.zero: Names a feature not in the dataset (e.g. 'renk/color', 'fiyat/price') or empty.

credit_rules.full: Numeric value plausible for the stated feature, with correct ≤ or > operator.
credit_rules.partial: Numeric value plausible but operator missing or wrong.
credit_rules.zero: Empty or value impossible for stated feature.

count_items.arithmetic_check: For each row: left_count + right_count must equal total food cards used (typically 10). Misclassification count = foods on wrong side of threshold.

credit_rules.full: Count is a non-negative integer. Row totals sum correctly (left + right = total).
credit_rules.partial: Counts present but row total does not sum correctly.

credit_rules.full: MCR = misclassifications / total. Value is consistent with the count values in the same row.
credit_rules.partial: MCR formula attempted but computed incorrectly (arithmetic error, off by 1).

mcr_items.arithmetic_check: MCR must equal (misclassification_count) / total_cards. Flag if inconsistent.

credit_rules.full: Names a specific threshold value from the grid AND gives a data-based reason (e.g. 'lowest MCR', 'fewest misclassifications'). If multiple thresholds tied, states tie-breaking rule.
credit_rules.partial: Names a threshold but gives no reason, OR gives a reason but no specific threshold.
credit_rules.zero: Empty or reason is not data-based ('I prefer this one').

WS5_B25.tie_breaking_note: If student notes that multiple thresholds give equal MCR, any principled tie-breaking rule (e.g. smallest threshold, largest threshold, middle value) is acceptable.

blank_handling.B21_to_B24: These correspond to the 5th threshold trial. If blank, check if student's grid only has 4 rows filled. If 4 rows are complete and consistent, B21-B24 being blank is acceptable (student chose not to try a 5th threshold). Do NOT score as errors unless context indicates the student was supposed to complete 5 trials.
blank_handling.partial_grid_policy: Partial completion is acceptable if at least 2 threshold trials are filled with correct arithmetic.

overall_scoring.full: At least 4 complete rows with arithmetic consistency AND B25 with data-based reasoning
overall_scoring.partial: At least 2 rows complete with arithmetic OR B25 present but no complete rows
overall_scoring.zero: Fewer than 2 rows attempted or all MCR values absent
